operating_state: Total revenue reached $164.5 billion in 2024, representing 22% year-over-year growth driven by advertising revenue expansion across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp platforms. Ad impressions delivered increased 11% while average price per ad rose 10%, reflecting strong advertiser demand and improved targeting capabilities despite regulatory headwinds. Family Daily Active People grew 5% to 3.35 billion with worldwide ARPP increasing 15%, demonstrating broad-based user engagement growth across all regions. Reality Labs recorded substantial operating losses despite revenue growth, significantly impacting overall profitability.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $95.1 billion with research and development representing the largest component, driven by AI infrastructure investments and Reality Labs development costs. Cost of revenue rose 16% primarily from data center and technical infrastructure depreciation expense reflecting continued infrastructure expansion. Share-based compensation expense reached $16.7 billion compared to $14.0 billion in 2023, while marketing and administrative expenses declined from reduced restructuring charges. Employee headcount increased 10% to 74,067 employees focused on engineering and AI capabilities.
material_events: Meta initiated its first quarterly dividend program declaring $2.00 per share totaling $5.1 billion in payments to shareholders. The company repurchased $29.8 billion of Class A common stock with $51.3 billion remaining authorized for future repurchases. Long-term debt increased to $29.0 billion through new fixed-rate senior unsecured notes issued in August 2024. Board expanded to thirteen members with election of three new independent directors effective December 30, 2024.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation losses increased to $690 million from $366 million, reflecting heightened exposure to international economic volatility with 64% of revenue generated outside the United States. The Irish Data Protection Commission issued a EUR 1.2 billion GDPR fine currently under appeal with interim stay. European Commission opened formal DMA compliance proceedings challenging the company's consent-based advertising approach. Multiple jurisdictions implementing youth social media restrictions including Australia's under-16 ban effective December 2025.
forward_outlook: Management expects Reality Labs operating losses to increase significantly in 2025 from current levels as metaverse and AI investments accelerate. Capital expenditures projected at $60-65 billion in 2025 to support core business and generative AI infrastructure development. The company extended estimated useful lives of certain servers and network assets to 5.5 years beginning 2025, reducing future depreciation expense by approximately $2.9 billion. Effective tax rate projected at 12-15% for 2025 assuming current stock price levels and continued research tax credit benefits.
uncertainty_profile: FTC administrative proceeding seeks substantial business practice modifications including prohibition on minor data use for commercial purposes and potential Instagram and WhatsApp divestiture, with trial scheduled April 2025. IRS transfer pricing disputes involve potential additional federal tax liability up to $9.0 billion with Tax Court opinion expected in 2025. Multiple ongoing legal proceedings include European Commission competition investigations, extensive youth-related litigation alleging social media addiction, and privacy regulatory compliance reviews with potential for additional operational restrictions.
